title: Baton Capability Policy
description: The model-agnostic hard-eligibility contract for role model-selection. Declares (a) a capability taxonomy — the shared vocabulary a role's requirement and a model's provided set are both expressed in — and (b) each Baton role's required capabilities. A model may serve a role only if it provides every capability the role requires; eligibility is the intersection of the role's requirements and the engine registry's provided set. Baton owns the roles, so role→required-capability is a Baton contract; it replaces unportable model pins ("verifier = opus, for context") that name a model where they mean a capability and go stale every time the frontier moves. Baton declares the hard requirement ONLY — it does not rank or pick among eligible models; that is the engine's eval-based routing (a soft prior, never a gate). This is the front half of the golden thread: need → role → required-capability → eligible-model.

capability_token.description: A capability name; the interop token both a role requirement and a model's provided set reference. snake_case.

requirement.description: One required capability. A bare token requires a boolean capability be true. An object requires a threshold on a quantitative/ordinal capability.

oneOf.description: A boolean capability the role requires (must be provided === true).
A threshold requirement on a quantitative or ordinal capability, e.g. {"context_window": ">=200000"}. Exactly one key, whose name is the capability token and whose value is a comparator expression.

^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$.description: Comparator against the capability's provided value, e.g. '>=200000' (quantitative) or '>=high' (ordinal, compared by level index).

version.description: Artefact schema version for forward compatibility.

taxonomy.description: The capability vocabulary. Each key is a capability token; the engine's provider registry maps model metadata onto these same tokens. The taxonomy is the interop contract — a role requires a token; a model provides the same token; eligibility is the intersection.

kind.description: boolean: present-or-not (e.g. tool_calling). quantitative: a magnitude compared numerically (e.g. context_window). ordinal: a named level compared by its index in `levels` (e.g. reasoning_effort).

unit.description: For quantitative capabilities: the unit the provided value and any threshold are expressed in (e.g. 'tokens').

levels.description: For ordinal capabilities: the ordered levels, lowest first (e.g. ["low","medium","high"]). A threshold like '>=high' compares by index in this list.

roles.description: Per-role capability requirements. Keys are Baton role names. A model is eligible for a role iff it provides every requirement listed. Requirements are the HARD gate; ranking among the eligible is the engine's concern, not Baton's.

requires.description: Every capability the role requires. Empty is valid (no hard requirement). Each entry's capability token MUST exist in `taxonomy`.

rationale.description: Why the role needs these — the auditable link back to the rule that motivates the requirement (e.g. 'verifier requires large context because Rule 7 reasons over the full diff + tests + gates').

on_capability_absent.description: Contract for §3.5: what a capability-dependent gate does when an operator override routes it to a model that genuinely cannot provide the capability (with this policy in force, this is an override-only edge case). 'prose_fallback_gated': the gate must still return a verdict via a prose evaluation that still gates (never a silent skip). 'rule2_deferral': the gate records a Rule-2 capability-absent deferral (why + tracking + acknowledgement). Absent = engine default; declaring it makes the behaviour explicit rather than incidental.
